title: FEATURE REQUEST: podman manifest rm == podman rmi
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

Podman should uniformly handle manifest lists and images so as to cause less user-asonishment.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman rmi <manifest list>` != `podman manifest rm <manifest list>`

2. `podman push <manifest list>` != `podman manifest push --all <manifest list>`

**Describe the results you received:**

`podman rmi <manifest list>` does not always remove the manifest list itself.

`podman push <manifest list>` does not always push the manifest list itself.

**Describe the results you expected:**

`podman rmi <manifest list>` should act exactly the same as `podman manifest rm <manifest list>`
`podman push <manifest list>` should act exactly the same as `podman manifest push --all <manifest list>`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

v3.4

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

N/A

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

N/A

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

[Original discussion was relating to a docs update.](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11889#pullrequestreview-774230190)

comments.body:
> podman rmi <manifest list> should act exactly the same as podman manifest rm <manifest list>

I am not sure they should.  `podman rmi` currently tries to resolve to an instance (item) of the manifest list that matches the local platform.  Same for `push`.

IMHO manifest lists should be kept as much as possible out of `podman image`.
I agree with @vrothberg also i think that is what docker does. https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11889#discussion_r724720134

The problem is for users, since the CLI breaks down: "looks like a duck, but quacks like...a dog?"

IMHO, it's almost utterly useless to act on the local part of the manifest list.  Almost nobody is going to need to do that.  For 99.999% of all interaction with manifest list via it's FQIN, users will expect them to behave like images (i.e. commands should act on the manifest list, not the items).

One idea:  What about removing manifest lists from `podman images` entirely.  Force them to be interacted with only through the `podman manifest XYZ` part of the CLI.  Perhaps this breaks things vs docker...but it's much more POLA than the current arrangement.  It will also greatly simplify documentation.

@rhatdan @nalind ?

I think the president is to stay with Docker compatibility.  I could see throwing an error.
I could also adding a new field to podman images to show a list versus an image.

We've broken Docker-compat. before and I remember it caused a bunch of pain.  I would be in support of a warning or error when using the top-level sub-commands with a manifest list.  IMHO, the least surprising would probably be an error (vs taking unexpected actions).  The least disruptive would probably be a warning in addition to...

> I could also adding a new field to podman images to show a list versus an image.

I think this is reasonable.  Maybe something simple like a `[M]` at the beginning of the `IMAGE ID` value?  Though that may frustrate output-scraping tools, so would an unexpected column.  Maybe this would need to go with a `podman images --all` update or have a dedicated `--manifests=false|true` flag?

---

Another thought, I could reach out to the mailing-list, and ask if anyone in the community feels strongly about this and/or has other ideas about handling it.

> I think the president is to stay with Docker compatibility.

It's worth noting that `docker rmi` doesn't know about manifest lists created with `docker manifest`, and our original implementation used `rmi` to remove manifest lists before we added a `manifest rm` that did the same thing.
I think those are very salient points.  Thanks for sharing.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think I can close this issue. I believe we settled on the status quo was fine.  Reopen if I am mistaken.
